rationale: The page contains technical information on FASTag (RFID technology), Digital Twins (simulation and real-time data), and SSI Mantra (India's indigenous surgical robotic system). It also includes a large advertisement for a test series which was excluded from the cleaned text as branding noise.
cleanedText: About FASTAG - A device that employs Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) technology for making toll payments directly from account while the vehicle is in motion. - RFID consists of tags and readers and employs radio waves to communicate information of objects or people to nearby readers. - It is a short-range technology. - Affixed on the windscreen of the vehicle and enables a customer to make the toll payments directly from the account which is linked to FASTag. - Developed by: NPCI under National Electronic Toll Collection (NETC) program. 2.6.5. DIGITAL TWIN - A digital twin is a digital representation of a physical object, person, or process, contextualized in a digital version of its environment. - It can help an organization simulate real situations and their outcomes, ultimately allowing it to make better decisions. - It uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring. 2.6.6. SSI MANTRA SSI Mantra performed world’s first cardiac telesurgery across 10,000 km. About SSI Mantra - India’s indigenous surgical robotic system. - Key features: 3D High-Definition Visualization, Predictive Analytics, etc.
